hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'dies' would be 'diei' — 'dies' here is the nominative subject
hints.Accusative: While 'dies' can be accusative, here it is the nominative subject of 'solvet'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'dies' would be 'die' — 'dies' here is the nominative form

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'diei' ends in -ei
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'diei' ends in -ei

hints.Nominative: While 'irae' could be nominative plural, here it is genitive singular modifying 'dies'
hints.Dative: While 'irae' could be dative, here it is genitive singular meaning 'of wrath'
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'ira' would be 'ira' — 'irae' is the genitive singular

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'irae' ends in -ae

hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'irae' ends in -ae

hints.to solve: While 'solvere' can mean 'to solve' in English derivatives, the Latin means 'to dissolve/release'

hints.Present: Present 3rd sg. would be 'solvit' — 'solvet' has the -e- future marker of 3rd conjugation
hints.Imperfect: Imperfect 3rd sg. would be 'solvebat' — 'solvet' is future tense
hints.Perfect: Perfect 3rd sg. would be 'solvit' — 'solvet' has the -e- future marker

hints.servo, servare, servavi, servatum: These are the principal parts of 'servare' (to save/keep)
hints.volvo, volvere, volvi, volutum: These are the principal parts of 'volvere' (to roll)
hints.absolvo, absolvere, absolvi, absolutum: These are the principal parts of 'absolvere' (to absolve) — 'solvet' is from the simplex 'solvere'

hints.Nominative: While 'saeclum' could be nominative neuter, here it is the direct object of 'solvet'
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'saeclum' would be 'saecli' — 'saeclum' is nominative or accusative
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'saeclum' would be 'saeclo' — 'saeclum' is the accusative object of 'solvet'

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'saecli' ends in -i
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'saecli' ends in -i
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'saecli' ends in -i

hints.Nominative: While 'favilla' looks like nominative, here it follows 'in' and is ablative (in + abl. = 'in/into ashes')
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'favilla' would be 'favillae' — 'favilla' after 'in' is ablative
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'favilla' would be 'favillam' — 'favilla' here is ablative after 'in'

hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'favillae' ends in -ae
hints.3rd declension: 3rd declension nouns have genitive in -is — 'favillae' ends in -ae
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'favillae' ends in -ae

translation: The day of wrath, that day, will dissolve the world in ashes
